en.title: Teaching and Training
en.desc: Discover the art and science of teaching. Students explore learning theory, classroom management, and curriculum design while gaining real experience in local school placements — the first step toward a rewarding career in education.

postsec.name: BS in Education (Elementary / Secondary)
postsec.note: 4-year degree with state teacher licensure; required for public school teaching positions in most states.

postsec.name: Master of Arts in Teaching (MAT)
postsec.note: Graduate-level credential that increases earning potential and opens doors to curriculum leadership and administration.

postsec.name: Teach For America / TNTP
postsec.note: Alternative pathways into high-need school districts; participants earn a full teaching salary from day one.

postsec.name: Corporate Trainer / Workforce Instructor
postsec.note: AA or BS in HR, Education, or Communications; increasingly in-demand roles in healthcare, tech, and business.

careers.title: Classroom Teacher (K–12)
careers.desc: Educate and inspire students in public or private school settings across all grade levels and subject areas.

careers.title: Instructional Coordinator
careers.desc: Develop curriculum and instructional materials, and train teaching staff on implementation strategies.

careers.title: Corporate Trainer
careers.desc: Design and facilitate professional development, onboarding, and skills training programs for businesses.

careers.title: School Administrator
careers.desc: Lead school operations as a principal, assistant principal, or district-level administrator.

financial.name: TEACH Grant
financial.note: Up to $4,000/year for students who commit to teaching in high-need subjects or low-income schools for four years after graduation.

financial.name: Teacher Loan Forgiveness
financial.note: Up to $17,500 in federal student loan forgiveness after 5 consecutive years of teaching in qualifying Title I schools.

financial.name: Grow Your Own State Scholarships
financial.note: Many states offer scholarships for students who commit to teaching in the state after graduation — check your state's Department of Education.

financial.name: Federal Pell Grant
financial.note: Up to $7,395/year for eligible students. Apply via FAFSA at studentaid.gov.

es.title: Enseñanza y Capacitación
es.desc: Descubre el arte y la ciencia de enseñar. Los estudiantes exploran la teoría del aprendizaje, la gestión del aula y el diseño curricular mientras ganan experiencia real en escuelas locales.

postsec.name: BS en Educación (Primaria / Secundaria)
postsec.note: Título de 4 años con licencia docente estatal; requerido para posiciones de enseñanza en escuelas públicas.

postsec.name: Maestría en Artes en la Enseñanza (MAT)
postsec.note: Credencial de posgrado que aumenta el potencial de ingresos y abre puertas al liderazgo curricular.

postsec.name: Teach For America / TNTP
postsec.note: Caminos alternativos hacia distritos de alta necesidad; los participantes ganan un salario docente completo desde el primer día.

postsec.name: Capacitador Corporativo / Instructor Laboral
postsec.note: AA o BS en RRHH, Educación o Comunicaciones; roles cada vez más demandados en salud, tecnología y negocios.

careers.title: Maestro/a de Aula (K–12)
careers.desc: Educa e inspira a los estudiantes en escuelas públicas o privadas en todos los niveles y áreas temáticas.

careers.title: Coordinador Instruccional
careers.desc: Desarrolla currículos y materiales educativos, y capacita al personal docente en estrategias de implementación.

careers.title: Capacitador Corporativo
careers.desc: Diseña y facilita programas de desarrollo profesional, orientación y capacitación de habilidades para empresas.

careers.title: Administrador Escolar
careers.desc: Dirige las operaciones escolares como director, subdirector o administrador a nivel de distrito.

financial.name: Beca TEACH
financial.note: Hasta $4,000/año para estudiantes que se comprometan a enseñar en materias de alta necesidad durante cuatro años.

financial.name: Condonación de Préstamos para Maestros
financial.note: Hasta $17,500 en condonación de préstamos federales tras 5 años consecutivos de enseñanza en escuelas Title I.

financial.name: Becas Estatales Grow Your Own
financial.note: Muchos estados ofrecen becas para estudiantes que se comprometan a enseñar en el estado después de graduarse.

financial.name: Beca Federal Pell
financial.note: Hasta $7,395/año para estudiantes elegibles. Solicitar vía FAFSA en studentaid.gov.
